title: Variables, input, char and float
_comment: A lecture deck: an ordered playlist, not a copy of any content.
Slides of type 'ref' point at a page the BOOK already builds (chunk level
3 gives one page per subsection, which is exactly slide-sized), so the
activity, its coding window and its data file all come from the single
source of truth - change the book, the deck follows. Slides of type
'recap' / 'agenda' carry the short in-class-only text that has no place in
the book's prose. 'icebreaker' names one from decks/icebreakers.json.
Reconstructed from the retired LCS deck W1C2Variablesinputscharfloat.pdf.
Its per-term ephemera (that day's announcements, office-hour changes, the
'who in this class...' slide) are deliberately NOT here - those are
session-level, edited minutes before class without a rebuild.

slides.title: Where we left off
items: printf displays a message on the screen
\n starts a new line; quotes are escaped as \"
Comments: /* ... */ across lines, // to end of line

slides.title: What we learned
items: Variable names contain only letters, numbers and _
They must start with a letter or _
Variables must be initialized BEFORE they are used
